content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

A partitioning of the array AAA is the splitting of AAA into one or more non-empty contiguous subarrays such that each element of AAA belongs to exactly one of these subarrays.

Find the number of ways to partition AAA such that the parity of the sum of elements within the subarrays is alternating. In other words, if SiS_iSi​ denotes the sum of the elements in the iii-th subarray, then either


S1S_1S1​ is odd, S2S_2S2​ is even, S3S_3S3​ is odd and so on.
or S1S_1S1​ is even, S2S_2S2​ is odd, S3S_3S3​ is even and so on.
For example if A=[1,2,3,3,5]A = [1, 2, 3, 3, 5]A=[1,2,3,3,5]. One way to partition AAA is [1,2][3,3][5][1, 2] [3, 3] [5][1,2][3,3][5]. Another way to partition AAA is [1][2][3][3,5][1] [2] [3] [3, 5][1][2][3][3,5]. Note that there exists more ways to partition this array.

Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The array can be partitioned as follows

[1][2][3][1] [2] [3][1][2][3]
[1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3]

Test case 2: The array can be partitioned as follows

[4,4,4,4][4, 4, 4, 4][4,4,4,4]
